headline: Automated Tor sweeps planted webshells on Cisco Unified CM servers within days of a public proof of concept, the sharpest sign yet that the window between disclosure and exploitation has nearly closed.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers now weaponize freshly patched flaws faster than defenders deploy fixes, running automated Tor sweeps that drop webshells onto Cisco Unified Communications Manager servers within days of a proof of concept.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Cisco Unified CM Exploited via Tor Webshell Sweeps

emerging_trends.text: A single compromised integration at market-intelligence vendor Klue keeps spreading, with stolen OAuth tokens draining Salesforce data from BeyondTrust, LastPass, and more than a dozen other customers.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue Salesforce Breach Engulfs BeyondTrust and LastPass

emerging_trends.text: Malware authors have begun turning AI defenses against themselves, embedding nuclear and biological weapons text inside spyware to trip the safety guardrails that automated AI analysis relies on.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Malware Hides Behind AI Safety Guardrails

topics.title: Cisco Unified CM Exploited via Tor Webshell Sweeps
topics.latest_developments: Threat intelligence firm Defused reported its honeypots now see automated Tor sweeps abusing the WebDialer server-side request forgery to drop webshells and gain remote code execution on Cisco Unified CM servers.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-20230, a CVSS 8.6 server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager and its Session Management Edition, lets unauthenticated attackers reach internal services; Cisco patched it in early June 2026 and admins should apply the fix immediately.

sources.title: Cisco Unified CM flaw actively exploited to drop webshells (CVE-2026-20230)
Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
Hackers Exploiting Cisco Unified CM Vulnerability

topics.title: Klue Salesforce Breach Engulfs BeyondTrust and LastPass
topics.latest_developments: BeyondTrust and LastPass confirmed attackers stole their Salesforce data, pushing past a dozen the Klue customers verifying theft as the extortion group Icarus published stolen files.
topics.summary: Attackers breached market-intelligence platform Klue and used its stolen OAuth tokens to reach customers' Salesforce environments; affected firms should revoke Klue's tokens and review Salesforce access logs.

sources.title: BeyondTrust, LastPass Impacted by Klue-Salesforce Incident
LastPass customer data exposed through Klue supply chain attack
LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data

topics.title: CI/CD Flaws Let Anyone Hijack Open-Source Repos
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported newly disclosed CI/CD defects that let unauthenticated users seize control of millions of open-source repositories, deepening the pull-request supply-chain risk that Cordyceps exposed and that GitHub's actions/checkout update now blocks.
topics.summary: The flaws abuse continuous-integration workflows—the same pwn-request and Cordyceps patterns hitting Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache Doris, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python's Black—to inject code; maintainers should pin actions and lock down workflow triggers.

sources.title: Exploitable CI/CD Vulnerabilities Expose Millions of Repositories to Hijacking
'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns

topics.title: macOS Hit by ClickFix Infostealers and Security-Bypass Gap
topics.latest_developments: Two fresh macOS threats surfaced today: Dark Reading described a gap that lets ordinary users disable security and browser tools without admin rights or kernel exploits, and BleepingComputer detailed a ClickFix campaign that silently mounts malicious DMG files to plant infostealers.
topics.summary: Both techniques target macOS endpoints—one neutralizes defenses, the other delivers payloads through Terminal commands; teams should restrict Terminal abuse and monitor for silent disk-image mounts.

sources.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
New macOS ClickFix attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer

topics.title: Mistic Backdoor Feeds Ransomware Access Brokers
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek and BleepingComputer detailed Mistic, a stealthy backdoor that initial access broker Woodgnat, also tracked as KongTuke, runs to seed ransomware from Qilin, Interlock, Rhysida, Akira, 8Base, and Black Basta.
topics.summary: Mistic hits insurance, education, IT, and professional-services firms, handing operators a foothold they resell to multiple ransomware crews; defenders should hunt for the backdoor and broker activity.

sources.title: New 'Mistic' RAT Opens Door to Several Ransomware Families
Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke

topics.title: Malware Hides Behind AI Safety Guardrails
topics.latest_developments: Bruce Schneier flagged a malware developer who buries fake instructions about nuclear and biological weapons inside spyware comments, betting the policy-triggering text makes automated AI analysis refuse the file.
topics.summary: The trick exploits the AI safety guardrails defenders increasingly lean on to triage malware, arriving as The Hacker News warns of agentic adversaries that operate at machine speed; analysts should not depend on AI scanning alone.

sources.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary

weather: Thursday: Partly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 84F.

games.recap: Cole Young hits a two-run home run in the seventh to lift Mariners to a 3-2 victory over Pirates

business.title: Pittsburgh Council Votes on Skill-Games Tax
business.latest_developments: Pittsburgh City Council scheduled a preliminary and final vote Wednesday, June 24, on the skill-games tax that Councilman Anthony Coghill floated a week earlier.
business.summary: Pittsburgh City Council moved to tax the slot-like skill-games machines inside the city, acting ahead of Harrisburg after the Pennsylvania Supreme Court classified the devices as slot machines.

sources.title: Pittsburgh Council to vote Wednesday on bill to tax skill games

business.title: Westmoreland Cancels Park EV Chargers
business.latest_developments: Westmoreland County canceled its long-planned contract to install electric-vehicle charging stations in county parks.
business.summary: Westmoreland County dropped a project to place electric-vehicle chargers in its parks, ending a plan the county had pursued for years.

sources.title: Westmoreland County pulls plug on EV charging station contract

around_town.title: Sharpsburg Names Court for Greg Domian
around_town.latest_developments: Sharpsburg will dedicate the Kennedy Park basketball court to longtime community advocate Greg Domian.
around_town.summary: Sharpsburg is naming the Kennedy Park basketball court for Greg Domian, honoring his years of advocacy in the borough, with anticipation building locally around the dedication.

sources.title: Kennedy Park basketball court being named for longtime Sharpsburg advocate Greg Domian

around_town.title: Hempfield Upgrades Founders Park
around_town.latest_developments: Hempfield Township plans to spend up to $75,000 on fencing, signage, and other amenities at Founders Park.
around_town.summary: Hempfield Township will invest as much as $75,000 to improve the visitor experience at Founders Park with new fencing, signage, and added amenities.

sources.title: Hempfield plans $75K for visitor experience improvements at Founders Park

around_town.title: Brick Pitt Lego Shop Opens in Indiana Township
around_town.latest_developments: Bob and Tara Raposa grew their Lego side business into the Brick Pitt, an independent shop in Indiana Township.
around_town.summary: Bob and Tara Raposa run the Brick Pitt in Indiana Township, an independent retailer of Lego sets that gives collectors a local alternative to chain stores.

sources.title: The Brick Pitt offers Lego lovers an independent shopping choice in Indiana Township

events.title: Nia Sioux in CLO's 'Mean Girls'
events.latest_developments: Nia Sioux, the 'Dance Moms' alum, returns to Pittsburgh to perform with Pittsburgh CLO.
events.summary: Nia Sioux, who rose to fame on Lifetime's 'Dance Moms,' performs in Pittsburgh CLO's production of 'Mean Girls' at the Benedum Center, Downtown.

sources.title: 'Dance Moms' star Nia Sioux returns to Pittsburgh to perform at Benedum Center

around_teams.title: Ebron Joins Not Just Football
around_teams.latest_developments: Former tight end Eric Ebron appeared on Cam Heyward's Not Just Football to discuss his career and his retirement at 28.
around_teams.summary: On Not Just Football with Cam Heyward, ex-NFL tight end Eric Ebron recounted his time with the Lions, Colts, and Steelers, his retirement at age 28, and Bill Belichick's move to coach North Carolina.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

team_usa.title: USMNT's Diverse Roots
team_usa.latest_developments: A Guardian feature traced how the U.S. men's national team built its World Cup form from players of widely varied backgrounds.
team_usa.summary: The Guardian profiled the United States men's national team at the 2026 World Cup, arguing its strength grew from a patchwork of player backgrounds, cultures, and development paths, as the U.S. heads toward its Group D finale against Türkiye.

sources.title: From military brats to birthright citizens: how USMNT's magnificent mess became its strength

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: My Vibe Coding Adventure, The App and the Experience, Ten Takeaways
reading.summary: Ben Thompson recounts building an app he plans to use through 'vibe coding,' and draws ten takeaways about the experience and what it reveals about AI-assisted software development.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Ed Zitron argues the tech industry has fallen into cargo-cult behavior, imitating the rituals and trappings of past successes around AI while missing the underlying substance.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Cal Newport criticizes AI companies for stoking fears about the dangers of their own products, likening it to a carmaker publicly warning that its best-selling vehicle is too dangerous to drive.
